rounds.prompt: How many times deeper is the Mariana Trench than the Grand Canyon?
rounds.fact: The Grand Canyon is ~1,857 m deep; the Mariana Trench plunges 11,034 m — nearly 6× deeper.

rounds.prompt: How many times larger is the Pacific Ocean than the Mediterranean Sea?
rounds.fact: The Mediterranean covers ~2.5 million km²; the Pacific covers ~165 million km² — 66× larger by area, ~180× by volume.

rounds.prompt: How many times longer is the Amazon River than the River Thames?
rounds.fact: The Thames is ~346 km; the Amazon is ~6,992 km — about 20× longer, carrying 20% of all fresh water flowing into Earth's oceans.

rounds.prompt: How many times heavier is the water in all Earth's oceans than the Moon?
rounds.fact: Earth's oceans weigh ~1.4 × 10²¹ kg; the Moon weighs ~7.3 × 10²² kg — actually the Moon is ~52× heavier. But oceans are still ~1/52 of the Moon.

rounds.prompt: How many times taller is a tsunami wave than a typical ocean wave?
rounds.fact: A typical ocean wave is ~3 m high. The 1958 Lituya Bay megatsunami reached 524 m — nearly 175× taller.
